Purpose & context
Santiago is building DocRawl (v0.9.10, github.com/plater7/docrawl), a self-hosted web scraping tool that converts documentation websites into clean Markdown (or structured JSON) files using an LLM pipeline. The project follows a "simplicity wins" philosophy with backward compatibility and graceful failure handling as core principles. Santiago works as a software architect and uses an issue-driven, PR-based development workflow with conventional commits, feature branches, and GitHub CLI.

DocRawl technical reference

Stack: FastAPI, Playwright, markdownify, httpx, sse-starlette, slowapi, defusedxml, pydantic, Docker
Module structure:
  src/__init__.py
  src/api/__init__.py
  src/api/models.py - Pydantic models for API request/response.
  src/api/routes.py - API endpoints for crawl jobs.
  src/crawler/__init__.py
  src/crawler/discovery.py - URL discovery: sitemap, nav parsing, recursive crawl.
  src/crawler/filter.py - Deterministic URL filtering.
  src/crawler/robots.py - robots.txt parser.
  src/exceptions.py - Custom exceptions for Docrawl with user-friendly messages.
  src/jobs/__init__.py
  src/jobs/manager.py - Job management.
  src/jobs/runner.py - Job execution orchestration.
  src/jobs/state.py - Job state persistence for pause/resume (PR 3.1).
  src/llm/__init__.py
  src/llm/cleanup.py - LLM-based markdown cleanup with smart skip and dynamic timeouts.
  src/llm/client.py - LLM client supporting multiple providers: Ollama, OpenRouter, OpenCode.
  src/llm/filter.py - LLM-based URL filtering.
  src/main.py - FastAPI application entry point.
  src/scraper/__init__.py
  src/scraper/cache.py - Per-job page HTML cache with TTL and atomic writes (PR 2.4).
  src/scraper/converters/__init__.py - Converter registry for HTML -> Markdown backends.
  src/scraper/converters/base.py - MarkdownConverter Protocol (PR 3.4).
  src/scraper/converters/markdownify_converter.py - Default MarkdownConverter using markdownify (PR 3.4).
  src/scraper/converters/readerlm_converter.py - ReaderLMConverter: HTML -> Markdown via ReaderLM-v2/v1 (Ollama).
  src/scraper/detection.py - Blocked-response detection and content deduplication (PR 2.3).
  src/scraper/markdown.py - HTML to Markdown conversion, pre-cleaning, and chunking.
  src/scraper/page.py - Page scraping with Playwright — includes DOM noise removal and page pool.
  src/scraper/structured.py - Structured JSON output from HTML pages (PR 3.2).
  src/ui/__init__.py
  src/utils/__init__.py
  src/utils/security.py - Shared security utilities — SSRF validation.

LLM providers: Ollama (default, local); OpenRouter (OPENROUTER_API_KEY); OpenCode (OPENCODE_API_KEY); LM Studio (local)

  ADR-002-checkpoint-pause-resume.md - ADR-002: Checkpoint-Based Pause/Resume
  ADR-003-three-tier-cleanup-classification.md - ADR-003: Three-Tier LLM Cleanup Classification

  asyncio_mode: auto

Key learnings & principles

  Simplicity wins: New features should fail gracefully, maintain backward compatibility, and be opt-in with sensible defaults
  HTML pre-cleaning is critical: Removing framework noise before markdownify dramatically reduces chunk count and LLM processing time
  LLM model sizing matters: Oversized models for mechanical cleanup tasks cause severe performance degradation
  5-level fallback chain: lighter approaches first, escalate only on failure -- optimizes for common case
  PagePool reuse: ~40% scraping time reduction vs. open/close per URL
  3-tier cleanup classification: ~30% reduction in LLM calls by skipping code-heavy/short clean chunks
  Dynamic timeouts (BASE_TIMEOUT + per-KB scaling) prevent both premature failures and indefinite hangs
  Atomic file writes (.tmp then os.replace) for crash-safe state persistence

Approach & patterns

  Issue-driven development: GitHub issues then feature branches then PRs with conventional commits
  Uses Claude Code CLI for planning and implementation
  Prefers systematic, multi-phase analysis before implementation
  Testing approach: real documentation sites at ascending difficulty (httpx then FastAPI then Stripe then Cloudflare)
  Workflow: Ubuntu WSL2 environment, GitHub CLI for PR management
  Governance: Nebula for automation/monitoring, Claude Code for implementation, Notion for project planning
